news_items.text:
France vs Senegal lineups: Confirmed team news, predicted XIs, injury latest for World Cup today | The Standard

# France vs Senegal lineups: Confirmed team news, predicted XIs, injury latest for World Cup today

Didier Deschamps faces tough call over who to support Kylian Mbappe in star-studded Les Bleus attack

Arthur Ferridge, Sports Reporter@ArthurFerridge

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

France and Senegal both face selection dilemmas ahead of their Group I opener at the World Cup today.

Both sides will be hoping to get off to a strong start in New Jersey before rivals Norway and Iraq go head to head in Boston later in the day.

Didier Deschamps has led France to back-to-back World Cup finals but was handed a considerable fitness headache when Arsenal star William Saliba arrived in camp with a back injury, which some suggest may require surgery later this summer.

But Saliba is now back in training and should be fit for this challenging opening fixture against stripped Africa Cup of Nations champions Senegal.

Back in training: William Saliba

AFP via Getty Images

He will likely partner Bayern Munich’s Dayot Upamecano in the centre of defence, though Real Madrid star-to-be Ibrahima

# France starting lineup vs Senegal confirmed for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

Published: 2026-06-16T22:30:00+05:30
Source: khelnow.com (khelnow.com)
Language: en

## Story

France starting lineup vs Senegal confirmed for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

# France starting lineup vs Senegal confirmed for FIFA World Cup 2026 clash

Set As Preferred Source

Dilpreet has been covering football since 2020 and writing for Khel Now since 2025, covering World Football with a sharp perspective and a genuine passion for the game.

## This will be the second-ever clash between these nations.

The France national football team is now all set to begin their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign. For their opening game on the world stage, they are going to take on the Senegal national football team at the New York-New Jersey Stadium.

Didier Deschamps’ men are not going to have the first game in the flagship tournament. They are coming in after putting in decent performances in their friendly games ahead of the mega competition. They faced a defeat against Ivory Coast, but came out on top to secure a win agains

news_items.title: Senegal predicted XI v France: Mamadou Sarr in place of Kalidou Koulibaly? - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: Get French Football News Tue, June 16, 2026 at 8:25 AM UTC · 1 min read
news_items.text:
Senegal predicted XI v France: Mamadou Sarr in place of Kalidou Koulibaly? - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

TRENDING

World Cup: Mbappé, Messi in action

Sorsby turns to NFL

NBA Mock Draft 9.0

U.S. Open tee times

Story by

Senegal predicted XI v France: Mamadou Sarr in place of Kalidou Koulibaly?

Senegal will face France in their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener on Tuesday night in what is a repeat of the 2002 opener.

Pape Thiaw was in the squad that shocked Les Bleus 24 years ago and he will look to pull off another shock, this time as manager. Thiaw has a full squad to pick from. However, L’Équipe understands that Kalidou Koulibaly, who has returned from injury but may be lacking in sharpness, could miss out. It is Mamadou Sarr who could start in his place. The Chelsea defender’s pace against France’s attack may be favoured.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Idriss Gueye is another player returning from injury and he is expected to start in the Senegal midfield, alongside AS Monaco’s Lamine Camara.

## Senegal likely line-up v France

Edouard Mendy; El Hadji Malick Diouf, Moussa Niakhaté, Mamadou Sarr, Krépin Diatta; Pape Gueye, Idrissa Guey

# France vs Senegal lineups: Confirmed team news, predicted XIs, injury latest for World Cup today

Didier Deschamps faces tough call over who to support Kylian Mbappe in star-studded Les Bleus attack

Arthur Ferridge, Sports Reporter@ArthurFerridge

1 minute ago

COMMENTS

France and Senegal both face selection dilemmas ahead of their Group I opener at the World Cup today.

Both sides will be hoping to get off to a strong start in New Jersey before rivals Norway and Iraq go head to head in Boston later in the day.

Didier Deschamps has led France to back-to-back World Cup finals but was handed a considerable fitness headache when Arsenal star William Saliba arrived in camp with a back injury, which some suggest may require surgery later this summer.

But Saliba is now back in training and should be fit for this challenging opening fixture against stripped Africa Cup of Nations champions Senegal.

Back in training: William Saliba

AFP via Getty Images

Senegal predicted XI v France: Mamadou Sarr in place of Kalidou Koulibaly?

Senegal will face France in their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener on Tuesday night in what is a repeat of the 2002 opener.

Pape Thiaw was in the squad that shocked Les Bleus 24 years ago and he will look to pull off another shock, this time as manager. Thiaw has a full squad to pick from. However, L’Équipe understands that Kalidou Koulibaly, who has returned from injury but may be lacking in sharpness, could miss out. It is Mamadou Sarr who could start in his place. The Chelsea defender’s pace against France’s attack may be favoured.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Idriss Gueye is another player returning from injury and he is expected to start in the Senegal midfield, alongside AS Monaco’s Lamine Camara.

## Senegal likely line-up v France

Edouard Mendy; El Hadji Malick Diouf, Moussa Niakhaté, Mamadou Sarr, Krépin Diatta; Pape Gueye, Idrissa Guey"}
